identity.title: Prevent semantic mockups and remediate contract proof gaps

authorization.reference: Operator required Ferric Forager to remain a native Rust rewrite/reimagination that cannot use yt-dlp as product behavior, requested every finding be addressed with no more than six terra xhigh subagents, and directed Codex prevention changes first.

lifecycle.next_action: Remediate every validator finding with real public-boundary, mutation-sensitive proof and no product yt-dlp coupling.

scope.summary: Close the semantic-proof loopholes exposed by the WP-004/WP-005 inspection, make the Rust-only and non-product yt-dlp boundary unambiguous, and remediate the affected compatibility, lifecycle, graph, diagnostic, reporting, and validation behavior.
scope.zero_progress_declaration: This packet is design-authorized Phase 0 prerequisite remediation. It claims zero Ferric product, capability, runtime, packaging, release, replacement-parity, or phase progress. Every changed contract and proof surface must be consumed and re-proven by a future shipped Ferric entrypoint through FF-GATE-RUNTIME-001.
in_scope: Preserve and enforce the committed Codex prevention rules for product yt-dlp exclusion, proof independence, proof-class truth, wire authority, effect acknowledgement, graph cycles, and counterexamples
Make compatibility replay execute selected deterministic Rust semantics and fail closed for empty selections

Require every lifecycle external effect to receive an exact correlated acknowledgement, failure, or cancellation outcome before its target state is visible; keep failures and cancellations recoverable and retain explicit FFmpeg/filesystem domain states
Represent schema compatibility as explicit source-to-target authority and reject unrelated transitions, durable sequence zero, and undeclared diagnostic envelope fields
Reject prohibited SourceGraph relationship cycles deterministically within declared graph bounds
Replace mock architecture predicates and declaration-only proof with isolated mutations and actual public/composed boundary execution
Report declared proof support separately from evidence actually executed, with per-result proof metadata
Add exact independent counterexamples, including empty test bodies, indirect product wrappers, documentation-path assets, and permissive V1 schema migration
Run canonical and independent adversarial validation under the prerequisite ceiling
non_goals: Adding yt-dlp, Python, or a yt-dlp subprocess, wrapper, fallback, sidecar, package payload, or runtime service to Ferric product behavior
Creation of a Ferric downloader entrypoint
Claims beyond the explicit prerequisite ceiling
Repeating completed project research without a named stale-source trigger
Changing final product scope or the Rust plus FFmpeg and ffprobe dependency boundary
dependencies: Existing locked Rust workspace and approved dependencies only
Validated WP-003 build gates, WP-004 compatibility assets, WP-005 contracts, and WP-012 runtime-truth gate
yt-dlp remains limited to version-pinned non-shipped compatibility-oracle generation outside Ferric product execution

acceptance_matrix.requirement: Project Codex, build rules, and packet requirements prevent product yt-dlp coupling, structural checks mislabeled as behavioral replay, self-authored proof, proof-class promotion, and unacknowledged-effect completion.
evidence: product/clippy.toml compiler-resolved process and include guards

acceptance_matrix.requirement: Compatibility replay executes deterministic Rust semantics for every selected case, rejects empty selections, and keeps any offline yt-dlp oracle use non-shipped, version-pinned, provenance-bound research/build work.

Every lifecycle effect has correlated acknowledgement, failure, cancellation, and recovery semantics; no target, durable, release, or terminal state is visible before all required effects complete, including FFmpeg and filesystem effects.
Diagnostic negotiation accepts drift only through an explicit reviewed source-to-target transition; permissive V1 input grants no authority; durable sequence zero and undeclared fields are rejected.
SourceGraph rejects prohibited relationship cycles deterministically within declared graph bounds.

Independent public-boundary and isolated-mutation counterexamples fail when any corrected invariant is reverted; inventory proof identifiers resolve to executed behavior, not matching declarations or helper literals.
evidence: build/fixtures/architecture isolated compiled and public-boundary mutations

acceptance_matrix.requirement: Gate reports distinguish declared from executed proof classes and record per-result proof class, concrete input, boundary, expected result, observed result, and skipped semantic dependencies.
All listed canonical and independent checks must meet their defined expectations; FF-GATE-RUNTIME-001 must preserve the exact prerequisite zero-progress ceiling.
evidence: Prerequisite report records zero product, capability, phase, runtime, packaging, and release progress

checks_run: Independent validators reproduced the v1 activation-history defect and required supersession rather than backfill.
The exact non-product prerequisite declaration was added before the first committed non-STUB v2 checkpoint.
last_checkpoint.next_action: Commit this v2 activation checkpoint before further remediation.

non_product_prerequisite.required_future_proof: FF-GATE-RUNTIME-001 through the exact staged production artifact

required_outcomes: Native Rust Ferric behavior with no shipped yt-dlp dependency or indirect wrapper
Semantic proof cannot be substituted by fixture integrity, helper literals, static maps, or declaration equality
External effects have complete correlated outcome and recovery semantics before durable or terminal state
Compatibility drift requires explicit source-to-target authority
Previously accepted counterexamples are rejected through actual boundaries
risk_cases: A command labels an empty shard or hash/schema validation as semantic replay
A report promotes static proof declarations into executed evidence
A fixture validates a toy predicate instead of mutating and executing the governed boundary
A permissive V1 peer or empty authority token permits unrelated schema drift
A failed or cancelled external effect leaves an unrecoverable state
A documentation exception hides an executable or embedded product oracle wrapper
A named test remains declared but its behavior is emptied

reuse_candidates_in_order_with_result.candidate: no mechanism beyond the accepted outcome
reuse_candidates_in_order_with_result.result: Rejected because current proof labels and helper fixtures can pass without executing the governed boundary.
Selected for existing pure contracts, lifecycle engine, compatibility corpus, xtask reports, testkit, and canonical gates.
Selected for deterministic state, graph traversal, temp-copy mutations, path handling, and explicit allowlists.
Selected for supervised FFmpeg process intent only; yt-dlp and Python remain excluded from product execution.
Existing serde, serde_json, sha2, tempfile, and collections are sufficient.
Selected as explicit invariants plus boundary-executed mutation proof without new dependencies.

artifact_specific_justification.lifecycle: Make every emitted external effect outcome correlated and recoverable before durable or terminal state.
artifact_specific_justification.diagnostics: Replace peer self-authorization with explicit source-to-target compatibility authority and strict wire boundaries.
artifact_specific_justification.compatibility: Execute every selected case and reject empty replay selections.
artifact_specific_justification.proof_reporting: Separate declarations from executed evidence and include result-level proof metadata.
artifact_specific_justification.architecture_mutations: Exercise real isolated inputs and composed/public boundaries rather than local predicates.
artifact_specific_justification.product_boundary: Reject direct and indirect yt-dlp or Python product coupling, including hidden documentation assets.

change_evidence.pre_simplification_verdict: FAIL: independent validators reproduced empty semantic replay, static proof-class promotion, mock predicate fixtures, incomplete effect outcomes, a permissive V1 migration gap, and a bypassable documentation exception.
simplification_changes: Reused the existing pure Rust compatibility corpus and replaced label/digest replay claims with native per-plane semantic execution.
Reused one correlated lifecycle effect model for acknowledgement, failure, cancellation, recovery, and retry instead of adding adapter-specific shortcuts.
Reused the existing bounded graph model and one iterative traversal classifier for every traversable relationship kind.
Reused the gate report schema while deriving executed proof classes only from result rows and preserving exact prerequisite proof ceilings.
Kept yt-dlp confined to the pinned non-shipped research oracle and added compiler plus source-tree guards against direct or indirect product coupling.
change_evidence.post_simplification_verdict: PASS: native Rust boundaries, isolated mutations, exact non-ignored tests, and canonical gates now reject every reproduced proof-integrity defect without adding product yt-dlp or Python coupling.
complete_post_change_verification: Parse changed JSON, YAML, and TOML authority and fixture files
Verify AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md remain byte-identical
Reject an explicitly empty compatibility shard
Run isolated source/product mutations and the exact public-boundary counterexample with a required execution receipt
Inspect generated reports and verify executed proof classes and per-result metadata

ceilings_exceptions_and_evidence.current_ceiling: This packet repairs Phase 0 contract and proof prerequisites but no shipped Ferric entrypoint exists to prove product runtime behavior.
ceilings_exceptions_and_evidence.replacement_trigger: The first shipped Rust Ferric vertical slice consumes these contracts and executes representative media work.
ceilings_exceptions_and_evidence.likely_upgrade_path: Re-prove each consumed invariant through FF-GATE-RUNTIME-001 using the exact staged Ferric artifact and actual production boundaries.
ceilings_exceptions_and_evidence.trigger_detection_method: Classification scan, workspace-member inventory, manifest validation, and staged-execution validation fail closed.

DIFF_ATTACK_SURFACES: Contract and serialization boundaries: strict unknown fields, version authority, sequence bounds, and source-to-target schema transitions.
State and resource boundaries: correlated lifecycle effects, FFmpeg cleanup, filesystem confinement, durable prefixes, and stale retry generations.
Integration boundaries: complete compatibility corpus replay, isolated compiled mutations, public test boundaries, and Windows report paths.
Gate and product boundaries: result-derived proof classes, exact test execution, compiler-resolved process guards, and indirect oracle wrappers.
INDEPENDENT_CHECKS_RUN: Executed the complete native Rust compatibility replay and inspected all seven semantic rows plus their concrete inputs and boundaries.
Executed the public contract counterexample test and the exact graph relationship-cycle boundary as independent Rust tests.
Executed the full locked workspace tests, doctests, Clippy warnings-as-errors, and every inventory-mapped exact test.
Executed FF-GATE-DEEP-001 and FF-GATE-RUNTIME-001 and inspected their persisted proof-class and limitation metadata.
COUNTERFACTUAL_CHECKS: Removed the graph boundary implementation in an isolated workspace and required the independently compiled public test to fail.
Replaced mapped inventory behavior with a tautology and required both the source guard and independent behavior test to reject it.
Mutated replay expected labels and semantic rows and required native recomputation to reject self-attested outcomes.
Mutated gate report declarations and runtime classes and required serialized artifact validation to reject proof promotion.
BOUNDARY_PROBES: Probed zero, self-cycle, two-node cycle, exact node limit, and one-over graph relationship boundaries for every traversable edge kind.
Probed diagnostics sequence zero, undeclared wire fields, permissive V1 migration, unrelated transitions, and out-of-range protocol versions.
Probed stale, wrong-instance, wrong-effect, failed, cancelled, recovered, and retried lifecycle effect outcomes.
Probed lexical and canonical Windows report paths, empty replay shards, hidden documentation assets, and dynamically assembled process names.
NEGATIVE_PATH_CHECKS: An explicitly empty compatibility shard exits nonzero with FF-COMP-E-SHARD-EMPTY and cannot emit semantic PASS evidence.
Zero-test, ignored-test, and unrelated-test outputs fail the inventory exact-execution receipt parser.
Direct, indirect, embedded, dynamically assembled, Python, and yt-dlp product execution paths fail architecture or Clippy validation.
Failed or cancelled FFmpeg and filesystem effects remain nonterminal and require an exact newer-generation recovery acknowledgement.

INDEPENDENT_FINDINGS.finding: Structural fixture validation was mislabeled as behavioral replay; native per-plane Rust semantics now determine every replay row.
External effect failures and cancellations could bypass complete recovery; exact correlation and generation-aware retries now gate terminal state.
Peer-provided compatibility input could self-authorize schema drift; only immutable local directed transition authority now permits drift.
Relationship cycle validation did not cover every traversable edge kind; the bounded classifier now rejects each prohibited cycle deterministically.
An obsolete exact selector allowed a mutation command to exit successfully after running zero tests; the public boundary now requires one executed test.
Canonical Windows report paths could not be relativized against a lexical root; both sides are now canonicalized before containment proof.
The exact-test receipt rejected the normal text zero ignored and could not prove execution; it now parses one named passing non-ignored test.
Runtime gate results used a generic structural class while declaring different capabilities; each producer now records its actual result-level class.
Allowed policy and scenario proof classes serialized an aggregate of none; every allowed class now has an explicit weakest-evidence rank.
Change-evidence validation demanded a false pre-fix PASS for remediation work; truthful substantive FAIL history is now preserved and tested.
Parallel proof subprocesses could collide on nanosecond and process based capture names; an atomic sequence now guarantees per-process uniqueness.
Documentation exceptions could hide an embedded or indirect oracle wrapper; product scanning and compiler lints now reject those paths.

RESIDUAL_UNCERTAINTY: No shipped Ferric entrypoint exists yet, so media acquisition parity, cross-platform runtime behavior, packaging, and release readiness require future staged production-artifact proof.

MANUAL_REVIEW.reviewer: primary Codex adversarial reviewer after three independent Terra xhigh implementation lanes
MANUAL_REVIEW.method: Directly inspected the purpose, locked Cargo commands, yt-dlp boundary, failure recovery, and future runtime-proof sections.
evidence: The locked Cargo command surface names contract, diagnostics, core, testkit, deep, runtime, and PR workflows.
The recovery guidance covers report writes, strict wire failures, lifecycle acknowledgements, inventory errors, and data-only scan failures.
The manual states that FF-GATE-RUNTIME-001 must execute the exact staged production artifact before any Ferric runtime claim.
